errors: Missing the required 10-year CVD risk threshold logic: the CPG requires statin recommendation only when estimated 10-year CVD risk is **10% or greater** (and selective offer at **7.5% to less than 10%**), but the ELM does not compare `MostRecentValidCVDRiskAssessmentResult` against either threshold. Also, the ELM includes additional exclusion/contraindication logic not specified in the CPG (e.g., cirrhosis, ESRD/dialysis, rhabdomyolysis), and does not represent a general statin contraindication check.

[The “No Screening” logic appears to use a `ProcedureRequest` retrieval filtered by `orderedOn SameOrAfter Today`, which may not correctly represent “a chlamydia screening procedure request was ordered today or later (pending order)” if the intended order-date element is different; the ELM does not explicitly model a pending order status. Also, the “documented reason for not performing chlamydia screening” is implemented as an `Observation` code-based retrieve, which may be narrower than the CPG’s broader documented-refusal/exemption requirement.]

[ELM defines `Errors` with an `else` message ("Unable to generate weight screening recommendation...") when `MeetsInclusionCriteria` is true, but the CPG only specifies an error message when screening is not needed (`InPopulation = false`) and does not define any error output for the screening-needed case.]

Mammogram screening logic is incorrect: the CPG requires “no mammogram procedure within the past 1 year,” but the ELM uses `IsNull(ProcedureLookBack(...))`, which checks for a null list rather than absence of results. An empty result set would not necessarily be null, so this does not correctly implement the exclusion criterion.

Missing required qualifying encounter criterion: the CPG requires a qualifying encounter during the measurement period from the "Encounter to Screen for Depression" value set (`2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916`), but the ELM implementation does not reference or evaluate any encounter criterion.

[ELM does not exactly implement the female sex criterion: it checks `Patient.gender.value = "female"` instead of using the required “Female Administrative Sex” value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.560.100.2`. ELM does not exactly implement the DEXA exclusion logic: it checks only for a DEXA Observation with `issued` in the past 2 years, but the CPG requires a DEXA scan within the past 2 years **with a result present**. ELM does not exactly implement the osteoporotic fracture exclusion: it requires only a non-null `clinicalStatus` for matching Conditions, rather than correctly identifying a history of osteoporotic fractures from the specified value set.]

[Minimum Age mismatch: ELM uses age >= 25, but CPG requires age >= 18; Time interval mismatch: ELM uses a 6-month lookback for blood pressure screening encounters, but CPG requires 12 months (1 year); Therefore the inclusion/decision logic does not exactly implement the CPG screening criteria.]
[Age upper bound mismatch: CPG requires age <= 85 years inclusive, but ELM uses age <= 80 years; all other referenced criteria/logic appear consistent with the CPG]
HbA1c threshold mismatch: CPG requires most recent HbA1c Laboratory Test result >= 6.5%, but the ELM implementation compares against 9 g% (quantity value 9, unit "g%").

[ELM screening exclusion is incomplete: CPG requires a Pap Test “with a result present” performed within the past 3 years, but the ELM only checks for an Observation in the last 3 years by issued date and does not verify that a result is present. Also, the ELM rationale omits the hrHPV/cytology option for women aged 30-65, which is part of the stated CPG recommendation.]

[Smoking history criteria are incomplete: CPG requires active smoker or former smoker from the Tobacco User value set with at least 20 pack-years and quit within the past 15 years, but the ELM only checks for Tobacco User-coded Condition records with clinicalStatus = "active" and does not evaluate pack-years or quit-time. Also, the LDCT exclusion logic is not an exact match to the CPG because it retrieves Observation records and uses issued date, whereas the CPG specifies a low-dose CT scan performed within the past 1 year.]

[The ELM rationale does not exactly match the CPG-required rationale: it omits “ever smoked (100+ cigarettes in lifetime)” and “Grade B recommendation.” The smoking-history logic also appears mismatched: it retrieves `Condition` records using the “Tobacco User” value set, which does not clearly implement the required “ever smoked (100+ cigarettes in lifetime)” criterion.]

[Rationale text does not exactly match the CPG requirement: the ELM omits “including pregnant women” and “provide persons engaged in risky or hazardous drinking with brief behavioral counseling interventions. Grade B recommendation.”, and the “Has Recent Alcohol Screening” logic uses Observation.issued within 1 year rather than explicitly representing a screening performed within the past 1 year with a result present.]

[Rationale text does not exactly match the CPG: it omits "Grade B recommendation" and "Clinicians should assess fall risk annually."; the CPG requires the screening exclusion to be based on a falls risk assessment performed within the past 1 year with a result present, but the ELM uses Observation.issued as the timing field, which is not an exact match to "performed" date.]

[Rationale text does not match the CPG exactly: the CPG requires "USPSTF recommends that clinicians ask all adults about tobacco use, advise them to stop using tobacco, and provide behavioral interventions and U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA)-approved pharmacotherapy for cessation. Grade A recommendation." but the ELM only states "USPSTF recommends that clinicians ask all adults 18+ about tobacco use and provide behavioral interventions for cessation." ]

[Age lower bound mismatch: CPG requires age 45 to 75, but ELM uses age >= 60 to 75; Screening lookback mismatch: CPG requires no colorectal cancer screening within the past 1 year, but ELM checks for screening within the past 2 years; Recommendation logic/output mismatch: CPG specifies a full recommendation and rationale when InPopulation is true, but ELM only returns a truncated recommendation and sets Rationale to null]
[The “Has Recent Anxiety Screening” time window is 6 months in the ELM (`Today - 6 months`) but the CPG requires 1 year; this numeric interval does not match exactly.]

[Age criterion is incorrect: ELM uses age >= 30 years, but CPG requires age >= 15 years AND age <= 65 years. The upper age bound (<= 65) is missing, and the lower bound is wrong. Recommendation metadata is incomplete: when InPopulation is true, ELM returns a recommendation but no rationale (should include the USPSTF rationale); indicator and source are also not implemented.]

[BMI threshold mismatch: ELM defines "Is Obese" as BMI >= 35 kg/m2, but CPG requires BMI >= 25 kg/m2; Recent test interval mismatch: ELM checks for blood glucose/HbA1c tests within the past 1 year, but CPG requires within the past 3 years; Test result requirement missing: CPG requires the prior blood glucose/HbA1c test to have a result present, but the ELM only checks for the test date and does not verify result presence; Recommendation metadata mismatch: when screening is needed, CPG requires a non-null rationale, but ELM returns null for Rationale]

[Age lower bound mismatch: CPG requires age >= 21 years, but ELM uses age >= 30 years in "In Demographic"; Pap test exclusion logic does not verify "result present" as required by CPG, it only checks for an Observation issued within the past 3 years]

[Missing qualifying encounter criterion from "Encounter to Screen for Depression" value set (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916); recent screening lookback interval is 3 months in ELM but CPG requires within the past 1 year; ELM checks only Observation "issued" date and non-null value, but CPG requires a depression screening with a result present within 1 year]
[Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >= 65 years, but ELM uses age >= 75 years in "In Demographic".]

[DEXA recency mismatch: CPG requires no DEXA scan within the past 2 years, but the ELM checks for a scan within the past 6 months; Female sex criterion is not implemented using the required Female Administrative Sex value set and instead compares `gender.value` to `"female"`; Osteoporotic fracture exclusion is not correctly implemented because the ELM only checks that `clinicalStatus` is not null rather than matching an active fracture diagnosis from the specified value set; Osteoporosis/Osteopenia exclusions are limited to `clinicalStatus.coding[0].code = "active"` and do not fully reflect the requirement to exclude existing diagnoses from the specified value sets]

[Age lower bound mismatch: ELM uses age >= 55 years, but CPG requires age >= 65 years; Smoking history logic does not correctly implement the required "ever smoked (100+ cigarettes in lifetime)" criterion because it retrieves FHIR Condition records with the Tobacco User value set and filters on clinicalStatus active/resolved instead of directly representing smoking history; Prior AAA screening exclusion is not implemented exactly as required because the ELM checks for Observation records with the AAA Screening Ultrasound value set and only verifies non-null value, rather than clearly representing any prior abdominal aortic aneurysm ultrasound screening ever performed]

[MeetsInclusionCriteria uses OR between Screening Age and Gender, but the CPG requires AND; Mammogram logic is implemented as IsNull(ProcedureLookBack(...)) which may be acceptable for “no procedure in past 1 year,” but the main decision logic is incorrect because all three criteria must be true.]

[Age criterion mismatch: CPG requires age >= 50 AND age <= 80, but ELM only checks age >= 50 and omits the upper bound of 80; Smoking history mismatch: CPG requires active smoker or former smoker from Tobacco User value set with at least 20 pack-years and quit within past 15 years, but ELM only checks for existence of a Condition in the Tobacco User value set with clinicalStatus = active, and does not evaluate pack-years or quit-within-15-years; Output rationale mismatch: CPG requires the exact rationale including "currently smoke or have quit within the past 15 years" and "Grade B recommendation," but ELM rationale omits these details]

[Rationale text does not match the CPG exactly: the ELM omits "Grade B recommendation." from the required rationale. The smoking-history logic is also not clearly aligned with the CPG because it retrieves `Condition` records rather than directly implementing the specified "Tobacco User" value set criterion.]

[Missing exclusion criterion: ELM does not include "No active Bipolar Disorder diagnosis" in the decision logic for Needs Depression Screening, even though the CPG requires it. The ELM defines Has Bipolar Diagnosis, but it is never used in the final AND/NOT population logic. This causes the implemented inclusion criteria to differ from the CPG. ]

[Colonoscopy decision logic is inverted: the CPG requires colonoscopy count in the past 1 month = 0 for inclusion, but the ELM defines "Colonoscopy" as count > 0 and then ANDs it into MeetsInclusionCriteria. This causes patients with a recent colonoscopy to be included and patients without one to be excluded. The 1 month lookback interval and age threshold (>= 50 years) match the CPG.]

[Logical inversion error: the CPG requires InPopulation = age >= 65 AND NO falls risk assessment within the past 1 year, but the ELM defines "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" as age >= 65 AND HAS Recent Falls Assessment, and sets InPopulation to that expression. This makes the CDS fire when screening is NOT needed and suppress it when screening IS needed.]

[Missing qualifying encounter criterion: CPG requires a qualifying encounter during the measurement period from "Encounter to Screen for Depression" value set (`2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916`), but the ELM has no encounter-based inclusion logic. Wrong exclusion logic: CPG requires no active Bipolar Disorder AND no active Depression Diagnosis, but the ELM uses `NOT (Has Bipolar Diagnosis AND Has Depression Diagnosis)` instead of `NOT Has Bipolar Diagnosis AND NOT Has Depression Diagnosis` (or equivalently `NOT (Has Bipolar Diagnosis OR Has Depression Diagnosis)`), so a patient with only one active diagnosis would incorrectly remain eligible.]

[MeetsExclusionCriteria is implemented with AND between "Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED)" and "Amiodarones", but the CPG requires ANY exclusion criterion to qualify, so this must be OR. This changes the decision logic and would fail to exclude patients with only CTD or only active amiodarone therapy.]

[Value set mismatch: CPG requires "Fall Risk Assessment" value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028`, but the ELM uses `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.564`; recent assessment logic otherwise matches the 1-year window and result-present requirement.]

[Value set reference mismatch: CPG requires "Alcohol Use Screening" value set `2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542`, but the ELM uses `2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278`; Rationale text mismatch: CPG specifies the full USPSTF rationale including pregnant women and brief behavioral counseling interventions, but the ELM rationale only includes the first sentence and omits the rest]
